edge_hypothesis: All existing Supertrend Claude specs test HIGH ADX thresholds: ADX>5 (permissive), ADX>15, ADX>20 (default), ADX>25, ADX>30, ADX>35 (strict). The thesis in every case is 'filter for strong trends.' But the system's data tells a different story: (1) RANGING is the dominant profit regime — MACD champion PF=2.06 in ranging, Supertrend ADX5 PF=1.61 in ranging; (2) ea9p5k2m achieved its BEST PF in TRANSITIONAL (PF=2.765, 12 trades) — the zone where ADX is moderate (12-25), trends are FORMING or DISSOLVING, not established. The untested thesis: a Supertrend flip when ADX is moderate (>12) captures a fundamentally different signal than when ADX is high (>25). High-ADX Supertrend flips catch trend CONTINUATIONS after established trends briefly pull back. Low-to-moderate ADX Supertrend flips catch REGIME TRANSITIONS — the moment a range breaks into a trend, or a trend exhausts and reverses into a range. These transitions are where the largest dislocations occur because most market participants are positioned for the old regime. On 4h timeframe, a Supertrend flip with ADX > 12 targets multi-day regime transitions. The 4h Supertrend indicator (7-period, 3.0 factor) covers ~28 hours of price action. When it flips direction with moderate ADX, it's detecting a meaningful structural shift, not noise. Combined with extreme R:R (8:1) and 5-bar time cap (20 hours), this captures only transitions that produce immediate follow-through. The second variant tests ADX>10 on 1h for faster intraday transitions. Invalidation: if PF < the ADX>20 default (PF ~1.22), regime transitions detected by low-ADX Supertrend flips are too noisy and the ADX filter's value is in EXCLUDING weak signals, not including them.

variants.name: supertrend_4h_transition_adx12_8to1_cap5
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip with ADX > 12 (moderate trend threshold) on ETH/4h. 8:1 R:R (2.0x ATR stop, 16.0x ATR TP) with 5-bar time cap (20 hours). ADX > 12 is below the standard 20 threshold — it admits signals when a trend is FORMING (ADX rising from 10 to 15-20) but has not yet established itself. This is the 'early mover' variant: enter the trend as it begins, not after it's confirmed. The risk is more false signals (ADX 12-20 is a noisy zone), but the reward is being positioned early in the trend before the crowd. On 4h, each Supertrend flip represents a meaningful structural change (not intrabar noise). The 5-bar cap (20 hours) ensures only immediate follow-through is captured. ea9p5k2m showed transitional PF=2.765 — this variant explicitly targets that same regime through Supertrend instead of EMA/RSI/ATR.
entry_long: Supertrend direction (SUPERTd_7_3.0) flips from bearish to bullish (structural trend reversal signal).
ADX_14 > 12 (moderate trend strength — trend is forming, not necessarily established).
entry_short: Supertrend direction (SUPERTd_7_3.0) flips from bullish to bearish (structural trend reversal signal).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 20 hours on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < ADX>20 default (PF ~1.22), the ADX 12-20 zone introduces too much noise and the standard ADX>20 filter is optimal
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — structural trend reversal on 4h
ADX_14 > 12 — moderate threshold targeting regime transitions, not established trends
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R with 5-bar time cap (20 hours)

variants.name: supertrend_1h_transition_adx10_7to1_cap6
variants.description: Supertrend flip with ADX > 10 (very low threshold) on ETH/1h. 7:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 10.5x ATR TP) with 6-bar time cap (6 hours). ADX > 10 is the most permissive ADX filter in the system (excluding ADX>5 which is effectively no filter). On 1h, this captures intraday regime transitions — moments when a 6-12 hour range begins to break. The Supertrend flip is the primary signal quality gate; the low ADX threshold is intentionally permissive to TEST whether the Supertrend flip alone is sufficient for profitability at extreme R:R, or whether ADX filtering is actually necessary. Comparison: if this variant's PF > ADX>5 variant (existing st5k2r8w), the ADX>10 floor removes the noisiest signals while keeping transition signals. If PF < ADX>5, the Supertrend flip alone IS the edge and any ADX filtering hurts.
entry_long: Supertrend direction (SUPERTd_7_3.0) flips from bearish to bullish.
ADX_14 > 10 (minimal trend strength — any directional movement qualifies).
entry_short: Supertrend direction (SUPERTd_7_3.0) flips from bullish to bearish.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit at market after 6 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — 6 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 at ADX>10, the Supertrend flip needs meaningful ADX confirmation (>15+) to produce signal quality
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 6 bars at market price.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — structural signal on 1h
ADX_14 > 10 — very permissive, testing whether Supertrend flip alone carries the edge
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — 7:1 R:R with 6-bar time cap
